title: hieracium pulmonarioides subsp. spelaeum
intro: Hieracium pulmonarioides subsp. spelaeum, also known as a mountain hawkweed or spelaeum hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. It is a perennial herb that grows in mountainous regions and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and supporting respiratory health, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Herbal preparations of Hieracium pulmonarioides subsp. spelaeum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and topical creams, but it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
